name: Sfogliatelle
recipeIngredient: 3 tablespoons Minced candied orange and citron peel
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift flour and salt into a bowl.
2. Cut in shortening with fingers until the mixture reaches a mealy consistency.
3. Add enough water (approximately 1/2 cup) to hold the mixture together. The dough should not be too hard or too soft, similar to egg noodle dough.
4. Knead well for several minutes. Form into a ball and refrigerate for 30 minutes to allow it to lose elasticity and roll out easily.
5. Roll dough very thin on a lightly floured board into a rectangular piece measuring about 28 x 24 inches.
6. Cut the edges evenly, then cut into 4 pieces lengthwise (28 x 6 inches).
7. Using a brush, spread melted shortening generously and evenly over one strip, top with the second strip, and spread again with shortening.
8. Repeat with the third and fourth strips, separating each strip by a layer of shortening. Let the shortening cool and set.
9. Using great care, roll the four strips together as for a jelly roll, starting from the shorter end. Wrap in wax paper and refrigerate about 15 minutes.
10. Remove wax paper and cut the ends evenly using a sharp knife. Cut the roll into 12 pieces (1/2 inch), approximately 12 1/2 inches long. Place on a lightly floured board and sprinkle lightly with flour.
11. Gently place the rolling pin at the center of each slice and roll out from the center toward the left. Return the rolling pin to the center and roll out from the center toward the right. The slice, formerly round, will be a larger oval shape.
12. Gently roll again from the center toward the top with one stroke and from the center toward the bottom with one stroke. The oval should measure about 7 1/2 inches at its longest part. Place each prepared oval on a lightly floured board, rolled side down.
13. Repeat steps 10-12 with the other pieces, arranging the finished ovals on the board side by side.
14. Prepare Filling: Preheat oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit. Drain ricotta through cheese cloth until quite dry. Place in a bowl and stir in the remaining ingredients, blending thoroughly.
15. Place about a tablespoon of ricotta mixture on the lower half of each oval. Wet the edges of the dough with a little egg white and fold over the filling, bringing the narrow edges together to form a shell-like shape. Press edges tightly to seal in the filling.
16. Brush each shell with melted shortening. Bake for about 15 minutes, then quickly brush more melted shortening over the shells using light, gentle strokes.
17. Close the oven door quickly, lower the heat to 350 degrees Fahrenheit, and bake another 20 minutes. Brush once more with shortening.
18. Lower the heat to 250 degrees Fahrenheit and bake for an additional 5 to 10 minutes until puffed up and golden brown with shell-like ribs or lines. Cool on a rack before dusting with confectioners sugar.
